message:
perf: fix Three.js memory leaks, cache layout rects, and refactor click-particles

Three.js geometry/material disposal (header-icon-3d, three-scene):
- Hoist geometry/material creation into useMemo so GPU resources are
  allocated once; add useEffect cleanup that calls .dispose() on unmount
- Share geometry instances across identical meshes (e.g. torus reused
  across all 7 petals in MicroFlower instead of 7 separate allocations)
- Eliminates unbounded GPU memory growth when navigating between pages

Cache getBoundingClientRect on mouseEnter (magnetic, project-card,
thread-card, writing-card):
- Previously called getBoundingClientRect() on every mousemove event,
  forcing synchronous layout recalculation (layout thrashing)
- Introduce rectRef that captures bounding rect once on mouseEnter and
  reuses for all subsequent mousemove calculations in that hover session
- Clear rectRef on mouseLeave so stale rects are never used

Click-particles singleton refactor (use-click-particles):
- Replace per-component overlay canvas with global singleton canvas and
  shared animation loop to eliminate DOM thrashing on navigation
- Track activeHookCount for lifecycle management; animation loop self-
  terminates when particles array is empty (no idle requestAnimationFrame)
- Listen for prefers-reduced-motion dynamically via MediaQueryList event
- Add friction coefficient and sqrt(alpha) size falloff for smoother
  visual decay

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
